notes: Eval 1 (min > max): Both configurations score 5/5 — the naming convention assertion is satisfied by both but the skill produces a more specific, informative test name.
Eval 2 (SelectStrategy): Skill version correctly identifies #pragma/using issue and reports green vs red. Baseline misses the 3-part naming convention (Select_DoublesValues is 2-part).

Eval 3 (DictionaryStrategy): Key differentiator is the #pragma warning disable instruction — baseline uses // TODO comment which doesn't suppress compiler warnings. Skill correctly follows its own guidance.
The naming convention assertion (eval 1) is a weak discriminator — both configurations structurally satisfy it even though quality differs. Consider checking name specificity.
With-skill runs take more time (+24.7s avg) because the skill prompts reading production code and docs/decisions before writing tests.
